description: Machine-readable command catalog for files-cli. Agents should consult per-domain skills under skills/ for workflow guidance; this catalog is for programmatic surface discovery.

global_flags.--api-key: Single-use API key for this command.
global_flags.--session-id: Single-use session ID for this command.
global_flags.--workspace-id: Scope this command to a specific workspace.
global_flags.--format: Output format: json, table, csv. Always pass json from an agent.
global_flags.--fields: Comma-separated list of field names to include in the response.
global_flags.--output-path: Write output to a file instead of stdout.
global_flags.--non-interactive: Do not prompt for user input.
global_flags.--reauthentication: Re-supply the session user's password for security-sensitive operations.
global_flags.--ignore-version-check: Skip the CLI version-check on startup.

pagination_flags.--cursor: Resume listing from a cursor returned by a previous page.
pagination_flags.--max-pages: Cap the total number of pages fetched.

commands.name: api-keys find-current
commands.description: Show information about current API key.  (Requires current API connection to be using an API key.)

parameters.name: --id
parameters.description: Api Key ID.

parameters.name: --user-id
parameters.description: User ID.  Provide a value of `0` to operate the current session's user.

parameters.name: --description
parameters.description: User-supplied description of API key.

parameters.name: --expires-at
parameters.description: API Key expiration date

parameters.name: --name
parameters.description: Internal name for the API Key.  For your use.

parameters.name: --aws-style-credentials
parameters.description: If `true`, this API key will be usable with AWS-compatible endpoints, such as our Inbound S3-compatible endpoint.

parameters.name: --path
parameters.description: Folder path restriction for `office_integration` permission set API keys.

parameters.name: --permission-set
parameters.description: Permissions for this API Key. It must be full for site-wide API Keys.  Keys with the `desktop_app` permission set only have the ability to do the functions provided in our Desktop App (File and Share Link operations). Keys with the `office_integration` permission set are auto generated, and automatically expire, to allow users to interact with office integration platforms. Additional permission sets may become available in the future, such as for a Site Admin to give a key with no administrator privileges.  If you have ideas for permission sets, please let us know.

parameters.name: --id
parameters.description: As2 Partner ID.

parameters.name: --enable-dedicated-ips
parameters.description: If `true`, we will use your site's dedicated IPs for all outbound connections to this AS2 Partner.

parameters.name: --http-auth-username
parameters.description: Username to send to server for HTTP Authentication.

parameters.name: --http-auth-password
parameters.description: Password to send to server for HTTP Authentication.

parameters.name: --mdn-validation-level
parameters.description: How should Files.com evaluate message transfer success based on a partner's MDN response?  This setting does not affect MDN storage; all MDNs received from a partner are always stored. `none`: MDN is stored for informational purposes only, a successful HTTPS transfer is a successful AS2 transfer. `weak`: Inspect the MDN for MIC and Disposition only. `normal`: `weak` plus validate MDN signature matches body, `strict`: `normal` but do not allow signatures from self-signed or incorrectly purposed certificates. `auto`: Automatically set the correct value for this setting based on next mdn received.

parameters.name: --signature-validation-level
parameters.description: Should Files.com require signatures on incoming AS2 messages?  `normal`: require that incoming messages are signed with a valid matching signature. `none`: Unsigned incoming messages are allowed. `auto`: Automatically set the correct value for this setting based on next message received.

parameters.name: --server-certificate
parameters.description: Should we require that the remote HTTP server have a valid SSL Certificate for HTTPS? (This only applies to Outgoing AS2 message from Files.com to a Partner.)

parameters.name: --default-mime-type
parameters.description: Default mime type of the file attached to the encrypted message

parameters.name: --additional-http-headers
parameters.description: Additional HTTP Headers for outgoing message sent to this partner.

parameters.name: --as2-station-id
parameters.description: ID of the AS2 Station associated with this partner.

parameters.name: --name
parameters.description: The partner's formal AS2 name.

parameters.name: --uri
parameters.description: Public URI where we will send the AS2 messages (via HTTP/HTTPS).

parameters.name: --public-certificate
parameters.description: Public certificate for AS2 Partner.  Note: This is the certificate for AS2 message security, not a certificate used for HTTPS authentication.

parameters.name: --source
parameters.description: Source path/glob.  See Automation docs for exact description, but this is used to filter for files in the `path` to find files to operate on. Supports globs, except on remote mounts.

parameters.name: --destinations
parameters.description: A list of destination paths. Use a trailing slash for folder destinations and omit it for file destinations.

parameters.name: --destination-replace-from
parameters.description: If set, this string in the destination path will be replaced with the value in `destination_replace_to`.

parameters.name: --destination-replace-to
parameters.description: If set, this string will replace the value `destination_replace_from` in the destination filename. You can use special patterns here.

parameters.name: --interval
parameters.description: How often to run this automation? One of: `day`, `week`, `week_end`, `month`, `month_end`, `quarter`, `quarter_end`, `year`, `year_end`

parameters.name: --path
parameters.description: Path on which this Automation runs.  Supports globs, except on remote mounts.

parameters.name: --legacy-sync-ids
parameters.description: A list of legacy sync IDs the automation is associated with. If sent as a string, it should be comma-delimited.

parameters.name: --sync-ids
parameters.description: A list of sync IDs the automation is associated with. If sent as a string, it should be comma-delimited.

parameters.name: --user-ids
parameters.description: A list of user IDs the automation is associated with. If sent as a string, it should be comma-delimited.

parameters.name: --group-ids
parameters.description: A list of group IDs the automation is associated with. If sent as a string, it should be comma-delimited.

parameters.name: --schedule-days-of-week
parameters.description: If trigger is `custom_schedule`. A list of days of the week to run this automation. 0 is Sunday, 1 is Monday, etc.

parameters.name: --schedule-times-of-day
parameters.description: Times of day to run in HH:MM format (24-hour). Required for `custom_schedule` triggers. Optional for `daily` triggers - if not set, runs at midnight UTC.

parameters.name: --schedule-time-zone
parameters.description: Time zone for scheduled times. Optional for both `custom_schedule` and `daily` triggers. If not set, times are interpreted as UTC.

parameters.name: --holiday-region
parameters.description: Skip automation on holidays in this region. Optional for both `custom_schedule` and `daily` triggers.

parameters.name: --always-overwrite-size-matching-files
parameters.description: Ordinarily, files with identical size in the source and destination will be skipped from copy operations to prevent wasted transfer.  If this flag is `true` we will overwrite the destination file always.  Note that this may cause large amounts of wasted transfer usage.  This setting has no effect unless `overwrite_files` is also set to `true`.

parameters.name: --always-serialize-jobs
parameters.description: Ordinarily, we will allow automation runs to run in parallel for non-scheduled automations. If this flag is `true` we will force automation runs to be serialized (run one at a time, one after another). This can resolve some issues with race conditions on remote systems at the cost of some performance.

parameters.name: --import-urls
parameters.description: List of URLs to be imported and names to be used.

parameters.name: --flatten-destination-structure
parameters.description: Normally copy and move automations that use globs will implicitly preserve the source folder structure in the destination.  If this flag is `true`, the source folder structure will be flattened in the destination.  This is useful for copying or moving files from multiple folders into a single destination folder.

parameters.name: --ignore-locked-folders

parameters.name: --legacy-folder-matching
parameters.description: DEPRECATED: If `true`, use the legacy behavior for this automation, where it can operate on folders in addition to just files.  This behavior no longer works and should not be used.

parameters.name: --overwrite-files
parameters.description: If true, existing files will be overwritten with new files on Move/Copy automations.  Note: by default files will not be overwritten on Copy automations if they appear to be the same file size as the newly incoming file.  Use the `always_overwrite_size_matching_files` option in conjunction with `overwrite_files` to override this behavior and overwrite files no matter what.

parameters.name: --note

parameters.name: --recipients

parameters.description: A list of paths to include in this bundle.

parameters.description: If true, this Share Link bypasses site-wide expiration rules. Only site admins may set this.

parameters.description: Id of Form Field Set to use with this bundle

parameters.name: --clickwrap-id
parameters.description: ID of the clickwrap to use with this bundle.

parameters.name: --code
parameters.description: Bundle code.  This code forms the end part of the Public URL.

parameters.name: --create-snapshot
parameters.description: If true, create a snapshot of this bundle's contents.

parameters.name: --description
parameters.description: Public description

parameters.name: --dont-separate-submissions-by-folder
parameters.description: Do not create subfolders for files uploaded to this share. Note: there are subtle security pitfalls with allowing anonymous uploads from multiple users to live in the same folder. We strongly discourage use of this option unless absolutely required.

parameters.name: --expires-at
parameters.description: Bundle expiration date/time

parameters.name: --finalize-snapshot
parameters.description: If true, finalize the snapshot of this bundle's contents. Note that `create_snapshot` must also be true.

parameters.name: --max-uses
parameters.description: Maximum number of times bundle can be accessed

parameters.name: --internal-name
parameters.description: Internal name for identifying this Share Link.

parameters.name: --note
parameters.description: Bundle internal note

parameters.name: --path-template
parameters.description: Template for creating submission subfolders. Can use the uploader's name, email address, ip, company, `strftime` directives, and any custom form data.

parameters.name: --path-template-time-zone

parameters.name: --require-share-recipient
parameters.description: Only allow access to recipients who have explicitly received the share via an email sent through the Files.com UI?

parameters.name: --send-one-time-password-to-recipient-at-registration
parameters.description: If true, require_share_recipient bundles will send a one-time password to the recipient when they register. Cannot be enabled if the bundle has a password set.

parameters.name: --send-email-receipt-to-uploader
parameters.description: Send delivery receipt to the uploader. Note: For writable share only

This field should only be used for ad-hoc (human) searching, and not as part of an automated process.

parameters.description: Search entire site?  If true, we will search the entire site.  Do not provide a path when using this parameter.  This is the same API used by the search bar in the web UI when running 'Search All Files'.  Search results are a best effort, not real time, and not guaranteed to match every file.  This field should only be used for ad-hoc (human) searching, and not as part of an automated process.

parameters.description: If set, sort records by the specified field in either `asc` or `desc` direction. Valid fields are `workspace_id`, `name` or `expires_at`.

parameters.name: --filter

parameters.name: --filter-lt

parameters.name: --mine
parameters.description: Only show requests of the current user?  (Defaults to true if current user is not a site admin.)

parameters.name: --path
parameters.description: Folder path on which to request the file.

parameters.name: --destination
parameters.description: Destination filename (without extension) to request.

parameters.name: --user-ids
parameters.description: A list of user IDs to request the file from. If sent as a string, it should be comma-delimited.

parameters.name: --group-ids
parameters.description: A list of group IDs to request the file from. If sent as a string, it should be comma-delimited.

parameters.name: --id
parameters.description: Request ID.

parameters.name: --sort-by
parameters.description: If set, sort records by the specified field in either `asc` or `desc` direction. Valid fields are `workspace_id`.

parameters.name: --filter
parameters.description: If set, return records where the specified field is equal to the supplied value. Valid fields are `restoration_type`.

parameters.name: --earliest-date
parameters.description: Restore all files deleted after this date/time. Don't set this earlier than you need. Can not be greater than 365 days prior to the restore request.

parameters.name: --prefix
parameters.description: Prefix of the files/folders to restore. To restore a folder, add a trailing slash to the folder name. Do not use a leading slash. To restore all deleted items, specify an empty string (`''`) in the prefix field or omit the field from the request.

parameters.name: --restoration-type
parameters.description: Type of restoration to perform. `files` restores deleted filesystem items. `users` restores deleted users and associated access/authentication records.

parameters.name: --restore-deleted-permissions
parameters.description: If true, we will also restore any Permissions that match the same path prefix from the same dates.

parameters.name: --restore-in-place
parameters.description: If true, we will restore the files in place (into their original paths). If false, we will create a new restoration folder in the root and restore files there.

parameters.name: --update-timestamps
parameters.description: If true, we will update the last modified timestamp of restored files to today's date. If false, we might trigger File Expiration to delete the file again.

parameters.name: --workspace-id
parameters.description: Workspace ID for a workspace-scoped restore. `0` means the default site-wide scope.

parameters.name: --sort-by
parameters.description: If set, sort records by the specified field in either `asc` or `desc` direction. Valid fields are `name`, `export_type` or `disabled`.

parameters.name: --filter
parameters.description: If set, return records where the specified field is equal to the supplied value. Valid fields are `disabled` and `export_type`.

parameters.name: --filter-prefix
parameters.description: If set, return records where the specified field is prefixed by the supplied value. Valid fields are `export_type`.

parameters.name: --id
parameters.description: Scheduled Export ID.

parameters.name: --name
parameters.description: Name for this scheduled export.

parameters.name: --export-type

parameters.name: --export-options
parameters.description: Report-specific options. `permission_audit` supports `group_by` with `user` or `path`.

parameters.name: --user-id
parameters.description: Site Admin user who receives the completed export e-mail.

parameters.name: --disabled
parameters.description: If true, this scheduled export will not run.

parameters.name: --trigger
parameters.description: Schedule trigger type: `daily` or `custom_schedule`.

parameters.name: --interval
parameters.description: If trigger is `daily`, this specifies how often to run the scheduled export.

parameters.description: If trigger is `daily`, this selects the day number inside the chosen interval.

parameters.description: Times of day in HH:MM format for schedule-driven exports.

parameters.name: --schedule-time-zone
parameters.description: Time zone used by the scheduled export.

parameters.description: If set, sort records by the specified field in either `asc` or `desc` direction. Valid fields are `created_at`, `status` or `siem_http_destination_id`.

parameters.name: --filter
parameters.description: If set, return records where the specified field is equal to the supplied value. Valid fields are `created_at`, `siem_http_destination_id` or `status`. Valid field combinations are `[ siem_http_destination_id, created_at ]`, `[ status, created_at ]`, `[ siem_http_destination_id, status ]` or `[ siem_http_destination_id, status, created_at ]`.

parameters.name: --filter-gt
parameters.description: If set, return records where the specified field is greater than the supplied value. Valid fields are `created_at`.

parameters.name: --filter-gteq
parameters.description: If set, return records where the specified field is greater than or equal the supplied value. Valid fields are `created_at`.

parameters.name: --filter-lt
parameters.description: If set, return records where the specified field is less than the supplied value. Valid fields are `created_at`.

parameters.name: --filter-lteq
parameters.description: If set, return records where the specified field is less than or equal the supplied value. Valid fields are `created_at`.

parameters.name: --sftp-insecure-ciphers
parameters.description: If true, we will allow weak and known insecure ciphers to be used for SFTP connections.  Enabling this setting severely weakens the security of your site and it is not recommend, except as a last resort for compatibility.

parameters.name: --sftp-insecure-diffie-hellman
parameters.description: If true, we will allow weak Diffie Hellman parameters to be used within ciphers for SFTP that are otherwise on our secure list.  This has the effect of making the cipher weaker than our normal threshold for security, but is required to support certain legacy or broken SSH and MFT clients.  Enabling this weakens security, but not nearly as much as enabling the full `sftp_insecure_ciphers` option.

parameters.description: If true, the trigger file will be sent at the end of a successful sync even when no files were transferred.

parameters.name: --url
parameters.description: URL for testing the webhook.

parameters.name: --method
parameters.description: HTTP method(GET or POST).

parameters.name: --encoding
parameters.description: HTTP encoding method.  Can be JSON, XML, or RAW (form data).

parameters.name: --headers
parameters.description: Additional request headers.

parameters.name: --body
parameters.description: Additional body parameters.

parameters.name: --raw-body
parameters.description: raw body text

parameters.name: --file-as-body
parameters.description: Send the file data as the request body?

parameters.name: --file-form-field
parameters.description: Send the file data as a named parameter in the request POST body

parameters.name: --use-dedicated-ips
parameters.description: Use dedicated IPs for sending the webhook?

parameters.name: --sort-by
parameters.description: If set, sort records by the specified field in either `asc` or `desc` direction. Valid fields are `name`.

parameters.name: --filter
parameters.description: If set, return records where the specified field is equal to the supplied value. Valid fields are `name`.

parameters.name: --filter-prefix
parameters.description: If set, return records where the specified field is prefixed by the supplied value. Valid fields are `name`.

parameters.name: --id
parameters.description: Workspace ID.

parameters.name: --name
parameters.description: Workspace name
